sakkhipuṭṭho.meaning: asked as an eyewitness; questioned face to face
sakkhipuṭṭho.summary: This is a compound meaning “questioned as an eyewitness”.
breakdown: An eyewitness; one who has seen with his own eyes (sa + akkhi “eye”).

sakkhissāmahaṁ.summary: This is a sandhi fusion of sakkhissāmi “I will be able” and ahaṁ “I”.
sakkhissāmahaṁ.gram: verb, future 1st person singular + pronoun (sandhi fusion)
breakdown: I will be able (future of sakkoti, √sak).
I. (The final -i and a- coalesce in sandhi.)

sakkhiṁ.meaning: an eyewitness; with one's own eyes
breakdown: Eye. So sakkhi: one who sees with his own eyes, a witness.

sakko.note: uncertain: sakko can also be the name of Sakka, lord of the devas; the lexicon here gives only “capable”.

sakkāya.meaning: the existing body; personality; the assumed self
sakkāya.summary: This compound names the “existing personality” which is wrongly believed to contain a permanent self.
breakdown: Being, existing (sat > sak before k by assimilation).
Body; collection. So sakkāya: the existing body-and-mind, the personality.
sakkāya.goenka: The lexicon notes that sakkāya points to the belief in some kind of permanent personality or self within this body-mind structure. Through vipassanā the meditator examines this apparent person directly and finds only impermanent, arising-and-passing phenomena, so the belief in a lasting self is dissolved by experience rather than by argument.

sakkāyadiṭṭhi.meaning: the illusion of self; belief in a permanent personality
sakkāyadiṭṭhi.summary: This is a compound meaning “view of the existing body”, i.e. the wrong view that there is a permanent self.
sakkāyadiṭṭhi.goenka: Sakkāyadiṭṭhi, the illusion of self, is the first of the fetters that bind beings to suffering. The lexicon glosses it simply as “illusion of self”: the deeply held belief that this body-mind process contains a permanent “I”. It is eradicated at the first stage of liberation, when the meditator directly experiences that the whole mind-matter structure is nothing but impermanent, arising-and-passing phenomena.

breakdown: A bird (run-on from the preceding glossary entry).
sakuṇo bird sakkacca.note: uncertain: this key is a glossary run-on combining the entry sakuṇo “bird” with the following headword sakkacca “respectfully, carefully”; the gloss belongs to sakkacca.

sakyamunī.meaning: the Sakyan sage; the Sage of the Sakyas (an epithet of the Buddha)
sakyamunī.gram: masculine noun, compound (nominative singular, metrically lengthened)
breakdown: The Sakyan clan, into which the Buddha was born.

sakādāgāmi.meaning: a once-returner; one who returns only once more
sakādāgāmi.summary: This names the second stage of liberation, one who will return to this world at most once more.
sakādāgāmi.goenka: The sakadāgāmī (once-returner) is the second of the four stages of liberation. Having further weakened craving and aversion through continued practice, such a meditator will take birth in this world at most one more time before attaining full liberation.

sallakkhetvā.meaning: having perceived; having observed; having considered

samacāgā.meaning: equal in generosity; matching in the quality of letting go

samadhigacchati.gram: verb, present tense 3rd person singular (saṁ + adhi + √gam)
breakdown: Goes (√gam). Together: comes fully to, attains.

samagganandī.meaning: rejoicing in concord; delighting in harmony

samaggarato.meaning: delighting in harmony; taking pleasure in concord
breakdown: Delighting in, devoted to (past participle of ramati).
Point, top; here forming samagga “united, harmonious”.

samaggā in.note: uncertain: this key is a glossary parsing artifact — the English word “in” from the gloss “in union” was attached to the headword samaggā; see the entry samaggā.

samajjābhicaraṇassa.meaning: of frequenting festive gatherings; of visiting fairs

samajjābhicaraṇaṁ.summary: Named in the suttas as one of the channels through which wealth drains away.

breakdown: Root meaning “to tell, declare” (with ā: akkhāti “relates”).
Agent adjective: one who tells, a relater.

samannāgamanahetu.meaning: by reason of engaging in; because of the manner of getting engaged in
breakdown: The manner of getting engaged in, being endowed with (saṁ + anu + ā + √gam).
Cause, reason; at the end of a compound: “by reason of”.

samannāgata.gram: past participle of saṁ + anu + ā + √gam
breakdown: Prefix: along, after (anu > ann before ā).
Come to; as past participle: “come to be furnished with”, endowed with.

samannāgatassa.meaning: of one endowed with; of one affected by, followed by
samannāgatassa.gram: past participle (genitive/dative singular) of saṁ + anu + ā + √gam
breakdown: Endowed with, possessed of (saṁ + anu + ā + √gam + ta).

samannāgato.meaning: endowed with; possessed of; followed by
samannāgato.gram: past participle (nominative singular) of saṁ + anu + ā + √gam
breakdown: Come (past participle of āgacchati). Together: come to be furnished with, endowed.
End, limit. Samanta: complete on every side; samantā: from/on all sides.

samappitaṁ.meaning: possessed of; endowed with; consigned to
breakdown: Delivers, applies (√ar). As past participle samappita: handed over to, endowed with.

samasīlina.meaning: equal in virtue; of the same moral disposition
breakdown: Having sīla, of virtuous disposition (from sīla “morality”).
Root “to go, stride” with gerund suffix: “having gone beyond”.

samattaṁ.gram: past participle of saṁ + √ap (nominative/accusative singular neuter)
breakdown: Root “to reach, attain” with past participle suffix: attained, accomplished.

samattena.meaning: by what is accomplished; with what is complete

samatthi.meaning: is equal to; there is (anything) equal
breakdown: Is, there is. (The two a-vowels merge in sandhi.)

samattā.gram: past participle of saṁ + √ap (nominative plural / feminine singular)
breakdown: Root “to reach, attain” with past participle suffix: attained, accomplished.

samavipākino.meaning: having the same result; ripening alike
breakdown: Having a result, ripening (from vipāka “result, fruition”).

samaṁ.gram: indeclinable adverb (accusative of sama used adverbially)

breakdown: Become, being (past participle of bhavati). Together: having come to be endowed with.

sambhatañca.meaning: and what has been stored up; and what is gathered, maintained
sambhatañca.gram: past participle (neuter accusative singular) + enclitic ca
breakdown: And. (The ṁ becomes ñ before c by sandhi.)

sambhataṁ.meaning: stored up; gathered together; provisions, accumulated wealth
sambhataṁ.gram: neuter noun / past participle of sambharati (nominative/accusative singular)
breakdown: Brought, supported, maintained (past participle of √bhar). Together: brought together, stored.
Root “to be, become”: coming into being, production.

sambhavaṁ is.note: uncertain: this key is a glossary parsing artifact — the English word “is” from the gloss “is produced” was attached to the headword sambhavaṁ; see the entry sambhavaṁ.

sambhavesī.meaning: seeking birth; due to be born

sambhavesī seeking.meaning: seeking birth; due to be born
sambhavesī seeking.note: uncertain: this key is a glossary parsing artifact — the English word “seeking” from the gloss was attached to the headword sambhavesī; see the entry sambhavesī.

breakdown: Root “to be, become” with past participle suffix: come into being, produced.
